case_name: Potable reuse polishing for tebuconazole and ibuprofen under high-recovery and concentrate-handling constraints
task_description: Recommend a ranked list of feasible polishing treatment process chains for surface-water-derived potable reuse targeting tebuconazole and ibuprofen while balancing high water recovery, concentrate handling limitations, and reliable finished-water quality.

input_data.scenario_background: A utility is upgrading an advanced water recycling scheme that uses highly treated surface-water-influenced secondary effluent as a source for indirect potable reuse. Monitoring has shown recurring low-µg/L tebuconazole and ibuprofen in the feed to the final polishing step, and the utility wants a barrier that is dependable for potable reuse. However, the site has a strong preference for very high product water recovery and has limited options for disposal of brine or spent regenerant, so technically strong membrane or regenerable sorption options are not automatically preferred.

treatment_goal.target_requirement: Finished water should provide robust polishing for trace organic contaminants suitable for potable reuse with stable, high-confidence performance and no reliance on difficult concentrate disposal.
secondary_goals: maintain very high net water recovery
minimize hazardous or difficult liquid residuals
keep operation manageable for a municipal advanced treatment team

core_unit_functions.ozonation: oxidize tebuconazole and ibuprofen and transform a broad range of trace organics
core_unit_functions.biologically_activated_carbon: adsorb residual organics, biologically polish biodegradable ozonation byproducts, and reduce DOC
core_unit_functions.uv_disinfection: provide final disinfection barrier for potable reuse

applicability_rationale: ozone is well suited for ibuprofen and can substantially reduce many pharmaceuticals and several pesticide residues without creating a reject stream
pairing ozone with biologically activated carbon improves overall polishing robustness and helps manage ozonation transformation products
the train supports near-complete water recovery because it does not require a continuous concentrate stream
for potable reuse, the combined oxidative and adsorptive-biological barrier is more robust than single-step adsorption alone under moderate DOC conditions
this option best balances contaminant control with the explicit constraint against difficult concentrate handling
potential_risks: bromate formation risk must be managed because bromide is present
ozone dose control and BAC monitoring are needed to avoid incomplete oxidation or early carbon exhaustion
tebuconazole removal may depend on adequate ozone exposure and downstream polishing performance

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation_sedimentation: remove particulate matter and reduce NOM load before adsorption
core_unit_functions.dual_media_filtration: polish solids and improve downstream PAC contact effectiveness
core_unit_functions.powdered_activated_carbon: adsorb tebuconazole and ibuprofen without producing a concentrate stream
core_unit_functions.ultrafiltration: retain PAC fines and suspended solids for stable finished water

applicability_rationale: PAC is a practical non-concentrating option for trace organic polishing when high water recovery is required
ibuprofen and hydrophobic pesticide-like compounds such as tebuconazole are generally amenable to activated carbon adsorption, although competition from DOC matters
this route avoids membrane brine and can be integrated into conventional surface water polishing infrastructure
it ranks below ozone-BAC because PAC performance can be more sensitive to DOC competition, dose control, and sludge production
potential_risks: higher PAC demand may be needed because DOC is moderately elevated
contaminant removal reliability depends on contact time, dose, and separation of spent PAC
sludge quantity increases and solids handling capacity must be available

core_unit_functions.ozonation: partially oxidize trace organics and improve downstream adsorbability or biodegradability
core_unit_functions.ceramic_or_media_filtration: remove particles and stabilize influent to GAC
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon: adsorb remaining tebuconazole, ibuprofen, and oxidation byproducts

applicability_rationale: ozone followed by GAC is a credible potable reuse polishing train with no liquid concentrate stream
GAC can provide a clear adsorptive barrier for tebuconazole and residual ibuprofen after oxidation
this route is technically strong but ranks slightly below ozone-BAC because non-biological GAC alone may require more frequent media replacement under the given DOC load
it remains attractive where the utility prefers simpler downstream carbon operation than explicitly managed BAC biology
potential_risks: carbon replacement frequency may increase due to DOC competition and oxidation byproduct loading
bromate control remains necessary during ozonation
if GAC is not biologically active enough, biodegradable byproducts may not be removed as effectively as in BAC systems

core_unit_functions.ultrafiltration: protect downstream membrane system from particulate fouling
core_unit_functions.nanofiltration_or_low_pressure_reverse_o: provide strong rejection of trace organics and broad dissolved contaminant control
core_unit_functions.uv_advanced_oxidation: add oxidation barrier for residual low-molecular-weight organics
core_unit_functions.stabilization: adjust treated water chemistry for distribution or recharge compatibility

applicability_rationale: membrane-based treatment is technically capable and commonly associated with high-confidence potable reuse barriers
NF or RO would likely achieve strong removal of tebuconazole and ibuprofen while also addressing other dissolved constituents
this option ranks lower because the scenario explicitly prioritizes very high recovery and limited concentrate disposal options
the need to manage membrane concentrate is a major mismatch even though contaminant removal performance is strong
potential_risks: concentrate management burden may be unacceptable at the site
achieving very high recovery increases scaling and fouling risk
membrane integrity monitoring and skilled operation are required

core_unit_functions.anion_exchange_or_specialty_resin_adsorp: sorb selected trace organic contaminants using synthetic resin media
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_polishing: provide backup adsorption and reduce residual organics

applicability_rationale: specialty resins can be technically feasible for some micropollutant removal applications and compact designs
a resin plus GAC polishing train could be configured to control residual trace organics

this option ranks last because resin systems often create challenging residuals through regenerant waste or off-site media replacement logistics, which conflicts with the stated concentrate and residuals constraint
for the specific contaminant pair and moderate DOC matrix, the benefit over carbon-centered options is not strong enough to justify the residuals handling complexity
potential_risks: spent regenerant or media disposal can be operationally difficult
competitive adsorption from background organics may reduce run length
performance data for resin selectivity toward this exact contaminant mixture may be less established than for ozone-carbon approaches

preferred_rank_pattern: non-concentrating oxidation-plus-carbon routes should rank near the top
PAC or GAC-based adsorption options should appear as plausible alternatives

must_reflect: high water recovery is a major decision driver
avoidance of brine or difficult liquid residual disposal should materially affect ranking
potable reuse requires robust multi-barrier reasoning rather than single-contaminant removal only

must_contain: why ozone-based treatment is attractive for ibuprofen and broader trace organic control
why activated carbon performance is influenced by moderate DOC in surface water
why membrane options are downgraded despite strong contaminant rejection
why bromate or oxidation-byproduct management matters when ozonation is proposed

should_mention: seasonal variability in source water matrix
